title: How do I know if my tribe is “federally recognized?”
meta-title: How do I know if my tribe is federally recognized?”
meta-description: You’ll be asked if you’re a member of a federally recognized tribe.
bite: You’ll be asked if you’re a member of a federally recognized tribe.
page_name: How do I know if my tribe is “federally recognized?”
page_topic: How do I know if my tribe is “federally recognized?”
excerpt: You’ll be asked if you’re a member of a federally recognized tribe. A federally recognized tribe is an Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, or community that’s acknowledged by the U.S. Department of the Interior as an Indian tribe.
content: You’ll be asked if you’re a member of a federally recognized tribe. A federally recognized tribe is an Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, or community that’s acknowledged by the U.S. Department of the Interior as an Indian tribe.
